findings.summary: handle_summary_command ships hardcoded mock stage results as its only production behavior
evidence: Lines 312-326: mock_results is not a fallback — it is the only code path; the comment says 'In real usage, this would be called with actual stage results' but no real path exists
The command's stated purpose is 'Generate pipeline summary (for testing)' in the help string, making this a CLI command that can only ever produce fictional output
findings.suggestion: Either remove the 'summary' subcommand from the public CLI if it is not a real capability, or wire it to read actual pipeline checkpoint/report data from --output-dir. The mock scaffolding belongs in tests, not in the production CLI handler.

findings.summary: AccuracyIssue, CompletenessGap, and ScanResults are defined in both repo_scanner.py and doc_models.py
evidence: repo_scanner.py lines 30-58 define @dataclass AccuracyIssue, CompletenessGap, ScanResults
doc_scanner.py imports and re-exports AccuracyIssue, CompletenessGap, ScanResults from doc_models.py
The two ScanResults types have different fields (repo version has statistics dict; doc version has scan_date and phase-specific lists) — callers face ambiguity about which to use
findings.suggestion: Consolidate: have repo_scanner.py import its scan result types from doc_models.py (or a dedicated scan_models.py shared by both), eliminating the parallel class hierarchy. Rename to disambiguate if semantics genuinely differ (e.g., RepoScanResults vs DocScanResults).

findings.summary: PromptComposer.compose_template contains hardcoded test-specific fragment detection and default headers in production code
evidence: Line 72: `if fragment_ref.endswith('#test_template'):` — literal test fragment name baked into production dispatch
Lines 80-82: `headers = ['## Section1', '## Section2']  # Default for tests` — test defaults embedded as production fallback
The overall dispatch is a string-matching chain (`'format_requirements' in fragment_ref`, `'content_requirements' in fragment_ref`, etc.) rather than a registry or protocol
findings.suggestion: Replace the ad-hoc string-matching fragment dispatch with a registry or enum mapping fragment type keys to builder functions. Remove the `#test_template` special case and default test headers from production code; test templates should be self-contained test fixtures.

findings.summary: get_manuscript_review_system_prompt() duplicates a long system prompt that also exists in the prompt loader, creating two divergent sources of truth
evidence: Lines 77-94: a 15-line literal system prompt string is the hard-coded fallback when PROMPT_SYSTEM_AVAILABLE is False
The same content is loaded via loader.get_system_prompt('manuscript_review') when available, meaning two separate maintained versions of the same prompt exist
The try/except ImportError on lines 60-66 plus duplicated content is a seam design that will cause the two prompts to diverge over time
findings.suggestion: Make the prompt file the single source; if the file is missing at runtime, raise a clear ConfigurationError rather than silently falling back to a hardcoded string. Alternatively, embed the fallback as a raw string constant in the prompts JSON file so there is one place to edit.

findings.summary: _format_stage_result calls _find_slowest_stage([result] * 2) as a self-comparison hack to detect bottlenecks
evidence: Lines 388-391: `self._find_slowest_stage([result] * 2)  # Mock comparison` — duplicates the single result into a list of two to satisfy the non-empty input requirement of _find_slowest_stage
_find_slowest_stage on two identical entries always returns a non-None value, so the condition degenerates to `result.duration > 10` with extra overhead
The comment '# Mock comparison' in production code signals unfinished implementation
findings.suggestion: Remove the self-compare hack. The bottleneck check only needs `result.duration > 10`; replace with `if result.duration > 10:` directly. If context-aware bottleneck detection (relative to pipeline average) is needed, accept total_duration and compute the threshold from it, not from a duplicated list.

findings.summary: collect_output_files separates .exists() and .is_dir() into named booleans with no reuse of the names
evidence: Lines 70-73: `exists_check = output_dir.exists()` / `is_dir_check = output_dir.is_dir()` are stored then immediately combined in the next line's `if not exists_check or not is_dir_check:`
Neither variable is used again; the split adds two intermediate names with no explanatory value
findings.suggestion: Collapse to `if not output_dir.exists() or not output_dir.is_dir():` directly. If the two conditions need separate log messages, keep them but log them; otherwise the intermediate variables are noise.

findings.summary: PipelineSummaryGenerator has three near-identical format methods (_format_text_summary, _format_json_summary, _format_html_summary) each re-walking the same summary fields
evidence: Lines 99-330: all three format methods iterate summary.stage_results, compute executed_stages, call _find_slowest/fastest, and handle log_file paths independently
Stage iteration logic in HTML (lines 276-292) and text (lines 130-134) is structurally identical but written twice
Any new summary field (e.g., test counts) must be added in all three methods
findings.suggestion: Extract a summary-to-dict normalizer that converts PipelineSummary into a plain dict once, then pass that dict to thin format-specific renderers. This makes the data shape the single source of truth and eliminates the N-format duplication.

findings.summary: ConversationContext.clear() has a six-line docstring describing behavior trivially readable from three lines of code
evidence: The method sets self.messages = [], self.estimated_tokens = 0, and increments self._clear_count — all visible at a glance

The docstring restates each of these operations in prose including 'Removes all messages from the conversation context and resets the estimated token count to zero. Increments the internal clear counter for usage statistics tracking.' and 'Returns: None'
This is a canonical AI-generated docstring pattern: restating rather than explaining
findings.suggestion: Trim to a single line: 'Clear all messages and reset token count.' The 'Returns: None' section adds no value on a void method and should be removed.
